$doc: Codex hook registration. Symlinked to ~/.codex/hooks.json (Codex does not rewrite it today; leos-doctor asserts it is still a symlink). Same exit-code contract as Claude: guard 43->2 (fail-closed on missing script), formatter 44->2 (fail-open), council 42->2 (fail-open). SessionStart runs the instruction injector, which prints global/AGENTS.md as additionalContext on stdout and always exits 0 (fail-open — never blocks session start). NOTE: hooks require one-time trust via /hooks; a `git pull` that changes THIS file may require re-trusting it. $CODEX_HOME + $HOME expand at execution so a relocated CODEX_HOME keeps working.

hooks.command: sh -c 'CODEX_HOME="${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}"; S="$CODEX_HOME"/hooks/bash-guard.py; P="$CODEX_HOME"/leos-python; [ -f "$S" ] && [ -x "$P" ] || exit 2; "$P" "$S"; ec=$?; [ "$ec" = 0 ] && exit 0; exit 2'

sh -c 'CODEX_HOME="${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}"; S="$CODEX_HOME"/hooks/format-on-edit.py; P="$CODEX_HOME"/leos-python; [ -f "$S" ] && [ -x "$P" ] || exit 0; "$P" "$S"; ec=$?; [ "$ec" = 44 ] && exit 2; exit 0'

sh -c 'CODEX_HOME="${CODEX_HOME:-$HOME/.codex}"; S="$CODEX_HOME"/council/bin/council.py; P="$CODEX_HOME"/leos-python; [ -f "$S" ] && [ -x "$P" ] || exit 0; "$P" "$S" hook; ec=$?; [ "$ec" = 42 ] && exit 2; exit 0'
